evals.name: write playwright test
evals.prompt: A user asks: write e2e tests for login and check basic accessibility on the page. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses Playwright, detects/starts the dev server, writes user-facing tests, checks accessibility basics, runs tests, and reports artifacts.
assertions: The output uses Playwright as the E2E framework.
The output says to detect or start the app/dev server before running browser tests.
The output tests user-facing login behavior rather than implementation details.
The output includes accessibility checks or assertions.
The output runs the tests and reports pass/fail plus useful artifacts such as traces/screenshots when relevant.

evals.name: generate browser tests from manual flow
evals.prompt: A user describes a manual browser flow: open /checkout, add an item, apply a coupon, and verify the total. Describe how to turn this into tests.
evals.expected_output: The response translates the flow into stable Playwright locators/actions/assertions, controls data setup, avoids brittle sleeps, and verifies totals.
assertions: The output translates each manual step into Playwright actions and assertions.
The output recommends stable locators such as role/text/test id rather than brittle CSS paths.
The output handles test data setup or deterministic fixture state.
The output avoids arbitrary sleeps in favor of web assertions or waiting for conditions.
The output verifies the coupon and total as observable user-facing behavior.
